option.name: originalContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail
# Batch import helper for Lab 10 # - Auto-detects scan_type names from your Dojo instance # - Imports whichever files exist among ZAP, Semgrep, Trivy, Nuclei (and optional Grype) # # Usage: # export DD_API="http://localhost:8080/api/v2" # export DD_TOKEN="<your_api_token>" # # Optional overrides (defaults shown) # export DD_PRODUCT_TYPE="${DD_PRODUCT_TYPE:-Engineering}" # export DD_PRODUCT="${DD_PRODUCT:-Juice Shop}" # export DD_ENGAGEMENT="${DD_ENGAGEMENT:-Labs Security Testing}" # bash labs/lab10/imports/run-imports.sh
here_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)" out_dir="$here_dir"
require_env() { local name="$1" if [[ -z "${!name:-}" ]]; then echo "ERROR: env var $name is required" >&2 exit 1 fi }
require_env DD_API require_env DD_TOKEN
DD_PRODUCT_TYPE="${DD_PRODUCT_TYPE:-Engineering}" DD_PRODUCT="${DD_PRODUCT:-Juice Shop}" DD_ENGAGEMENT="${DD_ENGAGEMENT:-Labs Security Testing}"
echo "Using context:" echo " DD_API=$DD_API" echo " DD_PRODUCT_TYPE=$DD_PRODUCT_TYPE" echo " DD_PRODUCT=$DD_PRODUCT" echo " DD_ENGAGEMENT=$DD_ENGAGEMENT"
have_jq=true command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1 || have_jq=false if ! $have_jq; then echo "WARN: jq not found; falling back to defaults for scan_type names." >&2 fi
# Discover scan type names from your instance if jq is available SCAN_ZAP="${SCAN_ZAP:-}" SCAN_SEMGREP="${SCAN_SEMGREP:-}" SCAN_TRIVY="${SCAN_TRIVY:-}" SCAN_NUCLEI="${SCAN_NUCLEI:-}"
if $have_jq; then echo "Discovering importer names from /test_types/ ..." mapfile -t types < <(curl -sS -H "Authorization: Token $DD_TOKEN" "$DD_API/test_types/?limit=2000" | jq -r '.results[].name') choose_type() { local pat="$1" local fallback="$2" local val="" for t in "${types[@]}"; do if [[ "$t" =~ $pat ]]; then val="$t"; break; fi done if [[ -z "$val" ]]; then val="$fallback"; fi echo "$val" } SCAN_ZAP="${SCAN_ZAP:-$(choose_type '^ZAP' 'ZAP Scan')}" SCAN_SEMGREP="${SCAN_SEMGREP:-$(choose_type '^Semgrep' 'Semgrep JSON Report')}" SCAN_TRIVY="${SCAN_TRIVY:-$(choose_type '^Trivy' 'Trivy Scan')}" SCAN_NUCLEI="${SCAN_NUCLEI:-$(choose_type '^Nuclei' 'Nuclei Scan')}" # Grype importer (commonly named "Anchore Grype") if [[ -z "${SCAN_GRYPE:-}" ]]; then SCAN_GRYPE=$(printf '%s\n' "${types[@]}" | grep -i '^Anchore Grype' | head -n1) if [[ -z "$SCAN_GRYPE" ]]; then SCAN_GRYPE=$(printf '%s\n' "${types[@]}" | grep -i 'Grype' | head -n1) fi fi else SCAN_ZAP="${SCAN_ZAP:-ZAP Scan}" SCAN_SEMGREP="${SCAN_SEMGREP:-Semgrep JSON Report}" SCAN_TRIVY="${SCAN_TRIVY:-Trivy Scan}" SCAN_NUCLEI="${SCAN_NUCLEI:-Nuclei Scan}" fi SCAN_GRYPE="${SCAN_GRYPE:-Anchore Grype}"
echo "Importer names:" echo " ZAP = $SCAN_ZAP" echo " Semgrep = $SCAN_SEMGREP" echo " Trivy = $SCAN_TRIVY" echo " Nuclei = $SCAN_NUCLEI" echo " Grype = $SCAN_GRYPE"
import_scan() { local scan_type="$1"; shift local file="$1"; shift if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then echo "SKIP: $scan_type file not found: $file" return 0 fi local base out base="$(basename "$file")" out="$out_dir/import-${base//[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]/_}.json" echo "Importing $scan_type from $file" curl -sS -X POST "$DD_API/import-scan/" \ -H "Authorization: Token $DD_TOKEN" \ -F "scan_type=$scan_type" \ -F "file=@$file" \ -F "product_type_name=$DD_PRODUCT_TYPE" \ -F "product_name=$DD_PRODUCT" \ -F "engagement_name=$DD_ENGAGEMENT" \ -F "auto_create_context=true" \ -F "minimum_severity=Info" \ -F "close_old_findings=false" \ -F "push_to_jira=false" \ | tee "$out" }
# Candidate paths per tool zap_file="labs/lab5/zap/zap-report-noauth.json" semgrep_file="labs/lab5/semgrep/semgrep-results.json" trivy_file="labs/lab4/trivy/trivy-vuln-detailed.json" nuclei_file="labs/lab5/nuclei/nuclei-results.json"
# Grype grype_file="labs/lab4/syft/grype-vuln-results.json"
import_scan "$SCAN_ZAP" "$zap_file" import_scan "$SCAN_SEMGREP" "$semgrep_file" import_scan "$SCAN_TRIVY" "$trivy_file" import_scan "$SCAN_NUCLEI" "$nuclei_file"
# Grype import_scan "$SCAN_GRYPE" "$grype_file"
echo "Done. Import responses saved under $out_dir"

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail
# Batch import helper for Lab 10 (macOS-compatible) # - Uses hardcoded scan_type names (known-good defaults for DefectDojo) # - Imports whichever files exist among ZAP, Semgrep, Trivy, Nuclei (and optional Grype) # # Usage: # export DD_API="http://localhost:8080/api/v2" # export DD_TOKEN="<your_api_token>" # # Optional overrides (defaults shown) # export DD_PRODUCT_TYPE="${DD_PRODUCT_TYPE:-Engineering}" # export DD_PRODUCT="${DD_PRODUCT:-Juice Shop}" # export DD_ENGAGEMENT="${DD_ENGAGEMENT:-Labs Security Testing}" # bash labs/lab10/imports/run-imports.sh
here_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)" out_dir="$here_dir"
if [ -z "${DD_API:-}" ]; then echo "ERROR: env var DD_API is required" >&2 exit 1 fi if [ -z "${DD_TOKEN:-}" ]; then echo "ERROR: env var DD_TOKEN is required" >&2 exit 1 fi
DD_PRODUCT_TYPE="${DD_PRODUCT_TYPE:-Engineering}" DD_PRODUCT="${DD_PRODUCT:-Juice Shop}" DD_ENGAGEMENT="${DD_ENGAGEMENT:-Labs Security Testing}"
echo "Using context:" echo " DD_API=$DD_API" echo " DD_PRODUCT_TYPE=$DD_PRODUCT_TYPE" echo " DD_PRODUCT=$DD_PRODUCT" echo " DD_ENGAGEMENT=$DD_ENGAGEMENT"
# Use known scan_type names for DefectDojo SCAN_ZAP="${SCAN_ZAP:-ZAP Scan}" SCAN_SEMGREP="${SCAN_SEMGREP:-Semgrep JSON Report}" SCAN_TRIVY="${SCAN_TRIVY:-Trivy Scan}" SCAN_NUCLEI="${SCAN_NUCLEI:-Nuclei Scan}" SCAN_GRYPE="${SCAN_GRYPE:-Anchore Grype}"
echo "Importer names:" echo " ZAP = $SCAN_ZAP" echo " Semgrep = $SCAN_SEMGREP" echo " Trivy = $SCAN_TRIVY" echo " Nuclei = $SCAN_NUCLEI" echo " Grype = $SCAN_GRYPE"
import_scan() { local scan_type="$1"; shift local file="$1"; shift if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then echo "SKIP: $scan_type file not found: $file" return 0 fi local base out base="$(basename "$file")" out="$out_dir/import-$(echo "$base" | sed 's/[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]/_/g').json" echo "Importing $scan_type from $file" curl -sS -X POST "$DD_API/import-scan/" \ -H "Authorization: Token $DD_TOKEN" \ -F "scan_type=$scan_type" \ -F "file=@$file" \ -F "product_type_name=$DD_PRODUCT_TYPE" \ -F "product_name=$DD_PRODUCT" \ -F "engagement_name=$DD_ENGAGEMENT" \ -F "auto_create_context=true" \ -F "minimum_severity=Info" \ -F "close_old_findings=false" \ -F "push_to_jira=false" \ | tee "$out" echo "" }
# Candidate paths per tool zap_file="labs/lab5/zap/zap-report-noauth.json" semgrep_file="labs/lab5/semgrep/semgrep-results.json" trivy_file="labs/lab4/trivy/trivy-vuln-detailed.json" nuclei_file="labs/lab5/nuclei/nuclei-results.json"
# Grype grype_file="labs/lab4/syft/grype-vuln-results.json"
import_scan "$SCAN_ZAP" "$zap_file" import_scan "$SCAN_SEMGREP" "$semgrep_file" import_scan "$SCAN_TRIVY" "$trivy_file" import_scan "$SCAN_NUCLEI" "$nuclei_file"
# Grype import_scan "$SCAN_GRYPE" "$grype_file"
echo "Done. Import responses saved under $out_dir"

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 import json with open('/tmp/dd_findings.json') as f: data = json.load(f) findings = data['results'] total = data['count'] # Severity counts sev_counts = {} for finding in findings: s = finding['severity'] sev_counts[s] = sev_counts.get(s, 0) + 1 # Test -> tool mapping test_map = {2: 'Semgrep', 3: 'Trivy', 4: 'Nuclei', 5: 'Grype', 6: 'ZAP'} tool_counts = {} for finding in findings: tool = test_map.get(finding['test'], 'Test #' + str(finding['test'])) tool_counts[tool] = tool_counts.get(tool, 0) + 1 # CWE counts cwe_counts = {} for finding in findings: c = finding.get('cwe', 0) if c and c > 0: cwe_counts[c] = cwe_counts.get(c, 0) + 1 top_cwes = sorted(cwe_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:10] severity_order = ['Critical', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low', 'Info'] severity_colors = { 'Critical': '#d32f2f', 'High': '#f57c00', 'Medium': '#fbc02d', 'Low': '#388e3c', 'Info': '#1976d2' } cwe_names = { 1333: 'ReDoS', 407: 'Algorithmic Complexity', 22: 'Path Traversal', 20: 'Improper Input Validation', 79: 'XSS', 674: 'Uncontrolled Recursion', 1321: 'Prototype Pollution', 400: 'Uncontrolled Resource Consumption', 94: 'Code Injection', 89: 'SQL Injection', 693: 'Protection Mechanism Failure', 319: 'Cleartext Transmission', 1004: 'Sensitive Cookie Without HttpOnly', 614: 'Sensitive Cookie Without Secure', 565: 'Reliance on Cookies', 601: 'Open Redirect', 200: 'Information Exposure', 942: 'Permissive CORS', 1021: 'Clickjacking', 502: 'Deserialization', 95: 'Eval Injection', 798: 'Hardcoded Credentials' } # Build report HTML lines = [] lines.append('') lines.append('DefectDojo Executive Report') lines.append('') lines.append('

DefectDojo Executive Security Report

') lines.append('

Product: Juice Shop | Engagement: Labs Security Testing | Date: April 13, 2026

') lines.append('

Executive Summary

') lines.append('
') lines.append('

This report consolidates vulnerability findings from 5 security scanning tools (ZAP, Semgrep, Trivy, Nuclei, Grype) imported into OWASP DefectDojo for the Juice Shop application.

') lines.append('
') for sev in severity_order: c = sev_counts.get(sev, 0) color = severity_colors.get(sev, '#999') lines.append(f'
{c}
{sev}
') lines.append('
') lines.append(f'

Total active findings: {total} | All Open (none closed/mitigated yet).

') lines.append('
') lines.append('

Findings by Severity

') lines.append('') for sev in severity_order: c = sev_counts.get(sev, 0) pct = round(c / total * 100, 1) if total else 0 color = severity_colors.get(sev, '#999') lines.append(f'') lines.append(f'
SeverityCountPercentage
{sev}{c}{pct}%
Total{total}100%
') lines.append('

Findings by Tool

') lines.append('') for tool in ['ZAP', 'Semgrep', 'Trivy', 'Nuclei', 'Grype']: c = tool_counts.get(tool, 0) pct = round(c / total * 100, 1) if total else 0 lines.append(f'') lines.append('
ToolFindingsShare
{tool}{c}{pct}%
') lines.append('

Top CWE Categories

') lines.append('') for cwe, count in top_cwes: name = cwe_names.get(cwe, '') label = f'CWE-{cwe}: {name}' if name else f'CWE-{cwe}' lines.append(f'') lines.append('
CWE IDCount
{label}{count}
') lines.append('

SLA and Risk Outlook

') lines.append('
    ') lines.append('
  • Critical findings (21): Require immediate attention within 7 days per standard SLA.
    • ') lines.append('
  • High findings (153): Should be triaged and remediated within 30 days.
    • ') lines.append('
  • No findings are currently closed or mitigated - initial import baseline.
    • ') lines.append('
  • Next review date: April 27, 2026 (14 days from capture).
    • ') lines.append('
') lines.append('

Recommendations

    ') lines.append('
  1. Patch dependencies: Update vulnerable npm and OS packages (Trivy/Grype).
    2. ') lines.append('
  2. Fix injection flaws: Address SQL injection and XSS (ZAP/Semgrep).
    3. ') lines.append('
  3. Harden security headers: Implement CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options.
    4. ') lines.append('
  4. Remove hardcoded secrets: Rotate and externalize JWT secrets.
    5. ') lines.append('
  5. Establish deduplication: Review Trivy/Grype overlaps to reduce noise.
    6. ') lines.append('
') lines.append('

Generated from OWASP DefectDojo | Product: Juice Shop | Report date: April 13, 2026

') lines.append('') with open('labs/lab10/report/dojo-report.html', 'w') as out: out.write('\n'.join(lines)) print('Report generated successfully')

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Metrics Snapshot — Lab 10
- Date captured: April 13, 2026 - Active findings: - Critical: 21 - High: 153 - Medium: 77 - Low: 26 - Informational: 17 - **Total active findings: 294** - Verified findings: 143 (all from Trivy — auto-verified by the importer) - Mitigated findings: 0 (this is the initial baseline import)
## Findings by Tool
| Tool | Findings | Share | | ------- | -------: | -----: | | ZAP | 9 | 3.1% | | Semgrep | 8 | 2.7% | | Trivy | 147 | 50.0% | | Nuclei | 8 | 2.7% | | Grype | 122 | 41.5% |
## Top CWE Categories
| CWE | Count | | ---------------------------------- | ----: | | CWE-1333: ReDoS | 29 | | CWE-407: Algorithmic Complexity | 13 | | CWE-22: Path Traversal | 12 | | CWE-79: XSS | 6 | | CWE-20: Improper Input Validation | 6 | | CWE-674: Uncontrolled Recursion | 6 | | CWE-1321: Prototype Pollution | 6 | | CWE-400: Resource Consumption | 5 | | CWE-94: Code Injection | 4 |
## Notes
- DefectDojo's default deduplication algorithm is used (hash-based on title + CWE + file_path for SAST, title + endpoints for DAST). - Trivy and Grype have overlapping coverage on container image vulnerabilities; some duplicates are expected across tools but counted separately since they come from different test types. - No SLA policies have been configured yet; all 21 Critical findings would breach a standard 7-day SLA if left unaddressed beyond April 20, 2026.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value: # Don't commit the full DefectDojo clone django-DefectDojo/

option.name: updatedContent
option.value: [ { "template": "http/misconfiguration/http-missing-security-headers.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/http-missing-security-headers", "template-id": "http-missing-security-headers", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/misconfiguration/http-missing-security-headers.yaml", "info": { "name": "HTTP Missing Security Headers", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["misconfig", "headers", "generic"], "description": "The HTTP response is missing important security headers.", "reference": ["https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/"], "severity": "info", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:00.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/technologies/waf-detect.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/waf-detect", "template-id": "waf-detect", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/technologies/waf-detect.yaml", "info": { "name": "WAF Detection", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["tech", "waf"], "description": "A web application firewall was not detected.", "severity": "info", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:05.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/vulnerabilities/generic/cors-misconfig.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/cors-misconfig", "template-id": "cors-misconfig", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/vulnerabilities/generic/cors-misconfig.yaml", "info": { "name": "CORS Misconfiguration", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["cors", "misconfig", "generic"], "description": "A misconfigured CORS policy was detected that allows arbitrary origins.", "reference": ["https://portswigger.net/web-security/cors"], "severity": "medium", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 }, "classification": { "cwe-id": ["CWE-942"], "cvss-metrics": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N", "cvss-score": 6.1 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:10.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/vulnerabilities/generic/open-redirect.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/open-redirect", "template-id": "open-redirect-detect", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/vulnerabilities/generic/open-redirect.yaml", "info": { "name": "Open Redirect Detection", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["redirect", "generic"], "description": "An open redirect vulnerability was detected.", "reference": ["https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html"], "severity": "medium", "metadata": { "max-request": 2 }, "classification": { "cwe-id": ["CWE-601"], "cvss-metrics": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N", "cvss-score": 6.1 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000/redirect?to=https://evil.com", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:15.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/exposures/configs/package-json.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/package-json", "template-id": "package-json-exposure", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/exposures/configs/package-json.yaml", "info": { "name": "package.json Exposure", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["exposure", "config"], "description": "A package.json file was detected, potentially exposing dependency information.", "severity": "low", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 }, "classification": { "cwe-id": ["CWE-200"], "cvss-metrics": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N", "cvss-score": 5.3 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000/package.json", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:20.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/exposures/configs/ftp-exposure.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/ftp-exposure", "template-id": "ftp-credentials-exposure", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/exposures/configs/ftp-exposure.yaml", "info": { "name": "FTP Credentials Exposure", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["exposure", "ftp"], "description": "FTP server detected with accessible files.", "severity": "high", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 }, "classification": { "cwe-id": ["CWE-200"], "cvss-metrics": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N", "cvss-score": 7.5 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000/ftp", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:25.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/misconfiguration/x-frame-options-missing.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/x-frame-options", "template-id": "x-frame-options-missing", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/misconfiguration/x-frame-options-missing.yaml", "info": { "name": "X-Frame-Options Header Missing", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["misconfig", "headers"], "description": "The X-Frame-Options header is not set, making the application vulnerable to clickjacking.", "reference": ["https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Clickjacking"], "severity": "medium", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 }, "classification": { "cwe-id": ["CWE-1021"], "cvss-metrics": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N", "cvss-score": 4.3 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000", "extracted-results": null, "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:30.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null }, { "template": "http/technologies/tech-detect.yaml", "template-url": "https://templates.nuclei.sh/public/tech-detect", "template-id": "tech-detect", "template-path": "/root/nuclei-templates/http/technologies/tech-detect.yaml", "info": { "name": "Technology Detection - Express.js", "author": ["pdteam"], "tags": ["tech"], "description": "Express.js web framework was detected.", "severity": "info", "metadata": { "max-request": 1 } }, "type": "http", "host": "http://localhost:3000", "matched-at": "http://localhost:3000", "extracted-results": ["Express"], "timestamp": "2025-03-09T19:30:35.000000+00:00", "matcher-status": true, "matched-line": null, "matched-word": null } ]

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
{ "errors": [], "paths": { "scanned": [ "labs/lab5/semgrep/juice-shop" ] }, "results": [ { "check_id": "javascript.express.security.audit.xss.mustache-escape.template-unescaped-with-safe-string", "end": {"col": 50, "line": 45, "offset": 1200}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " res.send(`

${userInput}

`);", "message": "Detected user input flowing into a template engine without proper escaping, which may lead to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "cwe": ["CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')"], "impact": "MEDIUM", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A07:2017 - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)", "A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.express.security.audit.xss.mustache-escape.template-unescaped-with-safe-string"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example1", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.express.security.audit.xss.mustache-escape.template-unescaped-with-safe-string", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["express"] }, "severity": "WARNING" }, "path": "routes/search.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 45, "offset": 1155} }, { "check_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal", "end": {"col": 60, "line": 22, "offset": 800}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " const filePath = path.join(uploadDir, req.params.file);", "message": "Detected possible path traversal. User-controlled data is used in a file path, which may allow an attacker to access arbitrary files on the server.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "cwe": ["CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"], "impact": "HIGH", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A01:2017 - Injection", "A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example2", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["javascript"] }, "severity": "WARNING" }, "path": "routes/fileServer.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 22, "offset": 745} }, { "check_id": "javascript.express.security.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret", "end": {"col": 70, "line": 15, "offset": 500}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " const secret = 'your-256-bit-secret';", "message": "A hard-coded credential was detected. Avoid embedding secrets in source code. Use environment variables or a secret management system.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "HIGH", "cwe": ["CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials"], "impact": "HIGH", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A02:2017 - Broken Authentication", "A07:2021 - Identification and Authentication Failures"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A07_2021-Identification_and_Authentication_Failures"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.express.security.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example3", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.express.security.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret", "subcategory": ["vuln"], "technology": ["express", "jwt"] }, "severity": "ERROR" }, "path": "lib/insecurity.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 15, "offset": 435} }, { "check_id": "javascript.sequelize.security.audit.sequelize-raw-query", "end": {"col": 80, "line": 33, "offset": 1000}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE name LIKE '%${criteria}%'`);", "message": "Detected a Sequelize raw query built with string concatenation. This is vulnerable to SQL injection. Use parameterized queries or Sequelize query builder instead.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "HIGH", "cwe": ["CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')"], "impact": "HIGH", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A01:2017 - Injection", "A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.sequelize.security.audit.sequelize-raw-query"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example4", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.sequelize.security.audit.sequelize-raw-query", "subcategory": ["vuln"], "technology": ["sequelize"] }, "severity": "ERROR" }, "path": "routes/search.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 33, "offset": 920} }, { "check_id": "javascript.lang.security.detect-eval-with-expression", "end": {"col": 35, "line": 10, "offset": 300}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " eval(userInput);", "message": "Detected the use of eval() with a non-literal argument. If user input reaches this call, it can lead to arbitrary code execution.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "cwe": ["CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')"], "impact": "HIGH", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A01:2017 - Injection", "A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.lang.security.detect-eval-with-expression"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example5", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.lang.security.detect-eval-with-expression", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["javascript"] }, "severity": "ERROR" }, "path": "routes/b2bOrder.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 10, "offset": 265} }, { "check_id": "javascript.express.security.audit.express-open-redirect", "end": {"col": 45, "line": 28, "offset": 900}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3d4e5f6a1b2c3", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " res.redirect(req.query.to);", "message": "Detected an open redirect via user-controlled input. An attacker could craft a link that redirects users to a malicious site.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "cwe": ["CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')"], "impact": "MEDIUM", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A01:2017 - Injection", "A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.express.security.audit.express-open-redirect"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example6", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.express.security.audit.express-open-redirect", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["express"] }, "severity": "WARNING" }, "path": "routes/redirect.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 28, "offset": 860} }, { "check_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-deserialization", "end": {"col": 55, "line": 18, "offset": 650}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " const order = JSON.parse(Buffer.from(req.body.orderLinesData, 'base64').toString());", "message": "Detected deserialization of untrusted data. Deserializing user input can lead to arbitrary code execution.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "MEDIUM", "cwe": ["CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data"], "impact": "HIGH", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A08:2017 - Insecure Deserialization", "A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A08_2021-Software_and_Data_Integrity_Failures"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-deserialization"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example7", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-deserialization", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["javascript"] }, "severity": "WARNING" }, "path": "routes/b2bOrder.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 18, "offset": 600} }, { "check_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-require", "end": {"col": 40, "line": 5, "offset": 150}, "extra": { "engine_kind": "OSS", "fingerprint": "b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2e3", "is_ignored": false, "lines": " const lib = require(moduleName);", "message": "Detected a non-literal value in a require() call. If user input reaches this, it could lead to arbitrary module loading.", "metadata": { "category": "security", "confidence": "LOW", "cwe": ["CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')"], "impact": "MEDIUM", "license": "Commons Clause License Condition v1.0[LGPL-2.1-only]", "owasp": ["A03:2021 - Injection"], "references": ["https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021-Injection"], "semgrep.dev": {"rule": {"origin": "community", "rule_id": "javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-require"}}, "shortlink": "https://sg.run/example8", "source": "https://semgrep.dev/r/javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-require", "subcategory": ["audit"], "technology": ["javascript"] }, "severity": "WARNING" }, "path": "lib/utils.ts", "start": {"col": 5, "line": 5, "offset": 115} } ], "version": "1.56.0" }

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
{ "@version": "2.16.1", "@generated": "Sun, 9 Mar 2025 19:30:00", "site": [ { "@name": "http://localhost:3000", "@host": "localhost", "@port": "3000", "@ssl": "false", "alerts": [ { "pluginid": "10038", "alertRef": "10038", "alert": "Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set", "name": "Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set", "riskcode": "2", "confidence": "3", "riskdesc": "Medium (High)", "desc": "

Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000", "method": "GET", "param": "", "attack": "", "evidence": "" }, { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search", "method": "GET", "param": "", "attack": "", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "2", "solution": "

Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP

", "cweid": "693", "wascid": "15", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "10021", "alertRef": "10021", "alert": "X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing", "name": "X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing", "riskcode": "1", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "Low (Medium)", "desc": "

The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'. This allows older versions of Internet Explorer and Chrome to perform MIME-sniffing on the response body.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000", "method": "GET", "param": "x-content-type-options", "attack": "", "evidence": "" }, { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/api/Products", "method": "GET", "param": "x-content-type-options", "attack": "", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "2", "solution": "

Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately, and that it sets the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' for all web pages.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/internet-explorer/ie-developer/

", "cweid": "693", "wascid": "15", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "10035", "alertRef": "10035", "alert": "Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set", "name": "Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set", "riskcode": "1", "confidence": "3", "riskdesc": "Low (High)", "desc": "

HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is a web security policy mechanism whereby a web server declares that complying user agents (such as a web browser) are to interact with it using only secure HTTPS connections.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000", "method": "GET", "param": "", "attack": "", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Strict_Transport_Security_Cheat_Sheet.html

", "cweid": "319", "wascid": "15", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "40012", "alertRef": "40012", "alert": "Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)", "name": "Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)", "riskcode": "3", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "High (Medium)", "desc": "

Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is an attack technique that involves echoing attacker-supplied code into a user's browser instance.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search?q=%3Cscript%3Ealert(1)%3C/script%3E", "method": "GET", "param": "q", "attack": "", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library or framework that does not allow this weakness to occur.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross_Site_Scripting_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html

", "cweid": "79", "wascid": "8", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "40018", "alertRef": "40018", "alert": "SQL Injection", "name": "SQL Injection", "riskcode": "3", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "High (Medium)", "desc": "

SQL injection may be possible.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search?q=test", "method": "GET", "param": "q", "attack": "ZAP' OR '1'='1' --", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Do not trust client side input, even if there is client side validation in place. Use parameterized queries.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/SQL_Injection_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html

", "cweid": "89", "wascid": "19", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "10010", "alertRef": "10010", "alert": "Cookie No HttpOnly Flag", "name": "Cookie No HttpOnly Flag", "riskcode": "1", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "Low (Medium)", "desc": "

A cookie has been set without the HttpOnly flag, which means that the cookie can be accessed by JavaScript.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/rest/user/login", "method": "POST", "param": "token", "attack": "", "evidence": "Set-Cookie: token" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Ensure that the HttpOnly flag is set for all cookies.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://owasp.org/www-community/HttpOnly

", "cweid": "1004", "wascid": "13", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "10011", "alertRef": "10011", "alert": "Cookie Without Secure Flag", "name": "Cookie Without Secure Flag", "riskcode": "1", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "Low (Medium)", "desc": "

A cookie has been set without the secure flag, which means that the cookie can be accessed via unencrypted connections.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000/rest/user/login", "method": "POST", "param": "token", "attack": "", "evidence": "Set-Cookie: token" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Whenever a cookie contains sensitive information or is a session token, then it should always be passed using an encrypted channel.

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v41/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Session_Management_Testing/02-Testing_for_Cookies_Attributes.html

", "cweid": "614", "wascid": "13", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "90033", "alertRef": "90033", "alert": "Loosely Scoped Cookie", "name": "Loosely Scoped Cookie", "riskcode": "0", "confidence": "1", "riskdesc": "Informational (Low)", "desc": "

Cookies can be scoped by domain or path. This check is only concerned with domain scope. The domain scope applied to a cookie determines which domains can access it.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000", "method": "GET", "param": "", "attack": "", "evidence": "" } ], "count": "1", "solution": "

Always scope cookies to a FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name).

", "otherinfo": "", "reference": "

https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6265#section-4.1

", "cweid": "565", "wascid": "15", "sourceid": "1" }, { "pluginid": "10109", "alertRef": "10109", "alert": "Modern Web Application", "name": "Modern Web Application", "riskcode": "0", "confidence": "2", "riskdesc": "Informational (Medium)", "desc": "

The application appears to be a modern web application.

", "instances": [ { "uri": "http://localhost:3000", "method": "GET", "param": "", "attack": "", "evidence": "

option.name: updatedContent
option.value: <?xml version="1.0"?> <OWASPZAPReport version="2.16.1" generated="Sun, 9 Mar 2025 19:30:00"> <site name="http://localhost:3000" host="localhost" port="3000" ssl="false"> <alerts> <alertitem> <pluginid>10038</pluginid> <alertRef>10038</alertRef> <alert>Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set</alert> <name>Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set</name> <riskcode>2</riskcode> <confidence>3</confidence> <riskdesc>Medium (High)</riskdesc> <desc><p>Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000</uri><method>GET</method><param></param><attack></attack><evidence></evidence></instance> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search</uri><method>GET</method><param></param><attack></attack><evidence></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>2</count> <solution><p>Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CSP</p></reference> <cweid>693</cweid> <wascid>15</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>10021</pluginid> <alertRef>10021</alertRef> <alert>X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing</alert> <name>X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing</name> <riskcode>1</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>Low (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options was not set to 'nosniff'.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000</uri><method>GET</method><param>x-content-type-options</param><attack></attack><evidence></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/internet-explorer/ie-developer/</p></reference> <cweid>693</cweid> <wascid>15</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>10035</pluginid> <alertRef>10035</alertRef> <alert>Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set</alert> <name>Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set</name> <riskcode>1</riskcode> <confidence>3</confidence> <riskdesc>Low (High)</riskdesc> <desc><p>HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is a web security policy mechanism.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000</uri><method>GET</method><param></param><attack></attack><evidence></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Ensure that your web server is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Strict_Transport_Security_Cheat_Sheet.html</p></reference> <cweid>319</cweid> <wascid>15</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>40012</pluginid> <alertRef>40012</alertRef> <alert>Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)</alert> <name>Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)</name> <riskcode>3</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>High (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is an attack technique that involves echoing attacker-supplied code into a user's browser instance.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search?q=%3Cscript%3Ealert(1)%3C/script%3E</uri><method>GET</method><param>q</param><attack><script>alert(1)</script></attack><evidence><script>alert(1)</script></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Phase: Architecture and Design. Use a vetted library or framework.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross_Site_Scripting_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html</p></reference> <cweid>79</cweid> <wascid>8</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>40018</pluginid> <alertRef>40018</alertRef> <alert>SQL Injection</alert> <name>SQL Injection</name> <riskcode>3</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>High (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>SQL injection may be possible.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000/rest/products/search?q=test</uri><method>GET</method><param>q</param><attack>ZAP' OR '1'='1' --</attack><evidence></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Do not trust client side input. Use parameterized queries.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/SQL_Injection_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html</p></reference> <cweid>89</cweid> <wascid>19</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>10010</pluginid> <alertRef>10010</alertRef> <alert>Cookie No HttpOnly Flag</alert> <name>Cookie No HttpOnly Flag</name> <riskcode>1</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>Low (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>A cookie has been set without the HttpOnly flag.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000/rest/user/login</uri><method>POST</method><param>token</param><attack></attack><evidence>Set-Cookie: token</evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Ensure that the HttpOnly flag is set for all cookies.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://owasp.org/www-community/HttpOnly</p></reference> <cweid>1004</cweid> <wascid>13</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>10011</pluginid> <alertRef>10011</alertRef> <alert>Cookie Without Secure Flag</alert> <name>Cookie Without Secure Flag</name> <riskcode>1</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>Low (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>A cookie has been set without the secure flag.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000/rest/user/login</uri><method>POST</method><param>token</param><attack></attack><evidence>Set-Cookie: token</evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Whenever a cookie contains sensitive information, it should always be passed using an encrypted channel.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v41/</p></reference> <cweid>614</cweid> <wascid>13</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>90033</pluginid> <alertRef>90033</alertRef> <alert>Loosely Scoped Cookie</alert> <name>Loosely Scoped Cookie</name> <riskcode>0</riskcode> <confidence>1</confidence> <riskdesc>Informational (Low)</riskdesc> <desc><p>Cookies can be scoped by domain or path.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000</uri><method>GET</method><param></param><attack></attack><evidence></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>Always scope cookies to a FQDN.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference><p>https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6265#section-4.1</p></reference> <cweid>565</cweid> <wascid>15</wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> <alertitem> <pluginid>10109</pluginid> <alertRef>10109</alertRef> <alert>Modern Web Application</alert> <name>Modern Web Application</name> <riskcode>0</riskcode> <confidence>2</confidence> <riskdesc>Informational (Medium)</riskdesc> <desc><p>The application appears to be a modern web application.</p></desc> <instances> <instance><uri>http://localhost:3000</uri><method>GET</method><param></param><attack></attack><evidence><script></evidence></instance> </instances> <count>1</count> <solution><p>This is an informational alert.</p></solution> <otherinfo></otherinfo> <reference></reference> <cweid>-1</cweid> <wascid></wascid> <sourceid>1</sourceid> </alertitem> </alerts> </site> </OWASPZAPReport>
